channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kejriwal seeks clarity on E20 fuel impact from automakers over safety concerns
item.description: Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal has written to 29 automobile manufacturers to seek written clarification on the impact of E20 ethanol-blended fuel on vehicle mileage and engine safety. During a p...

item.title: McConnell's Hospitalization Threatens Trump's Military Spending Agenda
item.description: U.S. Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell (R-KY) remains hospitalized with no expected return to Congress, creating a critical obstacle for Donald Trump's military spending agenda. As chair of the S...

item.title: National Unity Through Shared Purpose: Lessons from England’s World Cup Victory
item.description: The article explores how England's World Cup victory against Mexico sparked a rare moment of national unity, highlighting the power of shared purpose over shared ancestry. It argues that collective pr...

item.title: Father Troy Bobbin, beloved parish priest, passes away after cancer battle
item.description: Father Troy Bobbin, the parish priest of Holy Spirit Catholic Church in Amaroo, has died after complications from cancer treatment. The Archdiocese of Canberra and Goulburn confirmed his passing at Ca...

item.title: Naira Depreciates to ₦1,379 Per Dollar
item.description: The Nigerian Naira continued its decline against the US Dollar, with the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) reporting an indicative exchange rate of ₦1,379 per dollar on July 8, 2026. This marks a slight i...

item.title: Nigel Farage Faces Credibility Crisis Amid Financial Scrutiny
item.description: Nigel Farage's political career faces significant challenges as he navigates financial scrutiny and questions about his credibility. The Reform UK leader resigned as an MP to trigger a byelection in C...

item.title: International News Roundup: Diplomatic Tensions, Constitutional Changes, and Health Crises
item.description: The article compiles multiple news stories from across Africa and beyond. Ghana has postponed South African President Cyril Ramaphosa's planned August visit due to rising xenophobia tensions, with off...

item.title: Seven injured in North York collision requiring hospitalization
item.description: A collision in North York has resulted in seven individuals requiring hospital treatment, according to local news reports. The incident, which occurred on July 7, 2026, involved a vehicle crash that s...

item.title: Moi University Admits 8,869 Students Amid Financial Recovery Efforts
item.description: Moi University is set to welcome over 8,800 first-year students after the Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) released its 2025 placement results. The university emphasi...

item.title: Experts Highlight Behavior Change for Sustainable Weight Loss
item.description: Experts emphasize that sustainable weight loss requires behavioral changes rather than extreme diets. They advise adopting habits like eating slowly, drinking water before meals, and staying active. N...

item.title: Comelec names UBJP and BFP as dominant parties for BARMM elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has officially recognized the United Bangsamoro Justice Party (UBJP) as the dominant majority regional parliamentary political party (RPPP) and the Bangsamoro Fed...

item.title: Filmmakers' Attempts to Adapt Homer's Odyssey: A Historical Overview
item.description: The article explores the rare instances where filmmakers have attempted to adapt Homer’s *Odyssey*, a tale known for its vast scope and complexity. It highlights The Simpsons’ six-minute retelling, wh...

item.title: Grab collaborates with EV charging companies to cut driver expenses
item.description: Grab, the leading ride-hailing platform in the Philippines, has announced a strategic partnership with EV charging firms to reduce operational costs for its drivers. This collaboration aims to provide...

item.title: Canadian Researchers Explore Liquid Biopsies to Detect Post-Treatment Cancer Recurrence
item.description: A Toronto-based research team is leading a five-year clinical trial to study liquid biopsies, a blood-based technology that detects traces of tumour DNA to improve cancer survivorship. The SHERLOCK pr...

item.title: PSX drops over 2,900 points amid market volatility
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange's (PSX) benchmark KSE-100 index experienced a significant decline of 2,939.51 points, or 1.58%, during intraday trading on Wednesday, ending at 183,316.04 points. This drop...

item.title: Ofcom fines Virgin Media £28m over customer cancellation mishandling
item.description: Ofcom has imposed a £28m fine on Virgin Media for systematically obstructing customers' attempts to cancel their contracts between January 2022 and September 2024. The regulator found that call centre...

item.title: KUCCPS Announces 2025 Placement Results with Over 293,000 Students Secured
item.description: The Kenya Universities and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) has finalized the 2026/2027 placement cycle, securing spots for 293,869 students in universities and tertiary institutions. Of th...

item.title: African Sovereigns Increasingly Use Total Return Swaps for Liquidity Amid Eurobond Challenges
item.description: African sovereign nations like Nigeria are increasingly relying on total return swaps (TRS) to secure foreign-currency liquidity as Eurobond yields remain high. This financial tool allows countries to...

item.title: Japan's Hayabusa2 Probe Captures Rare Image of Snowman-Like Asteroid Torifune
item.description: Japan's Hayabusa2 probe captured rare close-up images of near-Earth asteroid Torifune, revealing a snowman-like shape made of two joined lobes. The mission, which demonstrated the ability to deflect p...

item.title: Municipal Financial Mismanagement and Legal Cases Highlighted in Sowetan's Top Reads
item.description: Sowetan’s latest edition highlights critical issues facing South Africa. The first story focuses on Gauteng municipalities owing over R7bn to water boards and Eskom, with the National Treasury withhol...
